name: Outline
license: BSL-style / source-available with change date; review carefully
what: Collaborative team knowledge base with cleaner collaborative editing experience than a traditional wiki.
solves: Internal knowledge sharing, collaboration, and polished team docs.
users: Teams wanting modern collaborative docs with nicer UX than a classic wiki.
fit: Moderate fit, but BookStack is a stronger match for structured SOPs and simpler self-host operations. Outline is more attractive if collaborative editing experience is the priority.
runtime: Node.js web app with database, storage, search, and auth integrations.
components: Web app, relational DB, Redis/cache if configured, storage, search, and auth providers.
deploy: Self-hosting exists, but licensing and environment complexity are less friendly than BookStack.
deps: Database, storage, mail/auth provider, reverse proxy, and possibly Redis/search.
datastores: Relational DB plus file storage and optional cache/search.
auth: SSO/provider integrations and local/admin models depending on setup.
apis: Document APIs, auth integrations, and import/export surfaces.
security: Internal docs may be sensitive. License review and auth/storage hardening matter.
setup_path: Use only if collaborative editing UX outweighs licensing and ops simplicity concerns.
env: DB settings, app secrets, storage/auth config, base URL, and optional Redis/search.
persistence: Back up DB and file storage.
operations: License tracking, auth integration, storage, and upgrades need attention.
failure_points: Choosing Outline for internal SOPs when BookStack is simpler and more structured.
integration: Can serve as internal team knowledge base behind ZITADEL/SSO if collaborative editing is valuable.
verify: Decide whether collaborative knowledge editing is more important than structured SOP organization.
risk: Moderate due to license and overlap.
gdpr: Mostly internal docs; still personal data if users author/comment.
overlap: Strong overlap with BookStack for internal docs and with Docusaurus for public docs.
codex_read_first: Read the docs-platform overlap map before choosing Outline.
codex_best_path: Prefer BookStack unless Outline’s collaborative UX is explicitly required.
codex_never_assume: Never ignore source-available license terms when planning reuse or bundling.
